macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Stocks Hit Fresh Highs as Temporary US-Iran Peace Deal Boosts Markets
items.summary: US equities closed positive on June 29 following a temporary halt in attacks between the US and Iran and plans for Qatar negotiations, while European markets dipped despite tech sector support.

items.title: Bank of England and ECB Cut Interest Rates by 0.25% Amid Trade Tensions
items.summary: The Bank of England lowered its main rate to 4% (its fifth cut since August), while the ECB reduced rates for the eighth time as US tariffs and trade uncertainty threaten global growth.

items.title: World Bank Warns of Worst Global Economic Run Since 2008 Outside Recessions
items.summary: The World Bank cut growth forecasts for nearly 70% of economies globally, citing rising inflation, supply shocks, and trade tensions as key drivers of the downturn.

items.title: RBI Governor Malhotra: Central Bank Does Not Target Rupee Level
items.summary: Reserve Bank of India Governor Sanjay Malhotra stated the RBI does not target a specific rupee value, attributing recent depreciation to dollar demand while assuring robust forex reserves.

items.title: India's Core Sector Growth Flat in October, Weakest in 14 Months
items.summary: India's infrastructure sector showed zero year-on-year growth in October, reflecting mixed performances across eight core industries that account for over 40% of industrial output.

items.title: BIS Annual Report: Inflation Risks and AI Financing Vulnerabilities Persist
items.summary: The BIS 2026 Annual Economic Report highlights risks of ingrained inflation due to supply shocks and warns of stretched asset valuations and leveraged AI financing by hedge funds.

items.title: SCOTUS Expands Trump's Power Over Independent Agencies, Excluding Fed
items.summary: The Supreme Court expanded presidential authority over independent agencies but explicitly excluded the Federal Reserve, reinforcing its institutional independence.

items.title: Global Investment Divergence: China Adds Assets While Europe Stalls
items.summary: McKinsey reports investment has stalled in Europe and shifted in the US, while China adds three times more productive assets annually despite 40% lower capital returns.

items.title: S&P Global Outlook: Inflation Broadens as Energy Prices Feed to Consumers
items.summary: S&P Global's Q3 2026 outlook notes headline CPI inflation moving well above targets as higher energy prices feed through to final consumers amid a tug-of-war between AI investment and supply disruptions.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: J.P. Morgan Backs Crypto Clarity Act, Boosting Regulatory Confidence
items.summary: J.P. Morgan released a statement endorsing the U.S. Crypto Clarity Act, signaling strong institutional support for digital asset regulation and increasing the likelihood of the bill passing in July.

items.title: BlackRock Integrates Ethena in DeFi Push, ENA Token Surges 8%
items.summary: BlackRock expanded its DeFi presence by integrating Ethena, sending the ENA token up 8% as investors welcomed the high-profile institutional partnership.

items.title: MicroStrategy Announces New Framework to Sell Up to $1.25B in Bitcoin
items.summary: MicroStrategy unveiled a digital credit capital framework allowing it to sell up to $1.25 billion in Bitcoin under specific conditions to fund cash reserves, dividends, and buybacks, though no obligation to sell exists.

items.title: Bitcoin Holds Near $60,000 Despite ETF Outflows and Bearish Sentiment
items.summary: Bitcoin traded just above $60,000, a critical support level, amid $4 billion in monthly ETF outflows, capital rotation into AI, and negative sentiment with the fear and greed index at danger levels.

items.title: Soul Token Gains 2% as Market Shows Signs of Recovery
items.summary: Soul (SOL) rose 2% to $73, recovering from a prior dip to the $60s, indicating potential market stabilization despite broader bearish trends.

items.title: Japan's Crypto Consolidation Continues with SBI's $289M Bitbank Deal
items.summary: SBI paid $289 million (eight times revenue) for Bitbank, reflecting Japan's ongoing crypto market consolidation driven by regulatory changes and doubled custody ambitions.

items.title: Ethereum Fundamentals Strengthen Despite Price Lag
items.summary: Ethereum's fundamentals are improving with stronger network activity and institutional interest, even though its price has not yet caught up to the broader market trend.

items.title: Crypto Sentiment Hits Worst Level Since FTX Collapse
items.summary: Crypto sentiment reached its lowest point since the FTX collapse, with RSI at an all-time low, Google searches down, and the fear and greed index signaling danger, though major firms still forecast higher Bitcoin prices by year-end.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Google Unveils Massive Agentic AI Update to NotebookLM with Code Execution and Deep Research
items.summary: Google transformed NotebookLM into a powerful agentic AI research assistant featuring secure code execution, deep web research, video overviews, and auto-generated editable documents, with premium features locked to AI Ultra and Workspace business tiers.

items.title: IBM Crosses Sub-1nm Chip Barrier and Reports 25x Energy Efficiency Gains with NorthPole Inference Engine
items.summary: IBM Research achieved a breakthrough in digital chip manufacturing by crossing the sub-1nm barrier and demonstrated its NorthPole inference engine delivers 25x better energy efficiency and 5x better performance than comparable 12nm GPUs while reducing latency by 22x.

items.title: OpenAI Reclaims AI Coding Leadership as Token Costs Drop 100x Between Models
items.summary: OpenAI has reclaimed the AI coding crown, while industry analysis shows token costs plummeting—DeepSeek V4 Pro at $0.80 per million tokens versus Fable at $80, a 100x difference—with projections that next-year models will be 100x cheaper still.

items.title: Japan Enters AI Race with New Model Matching Claude Mythos Performance
items.summary: Japan officially joined the global AI competition by releasing a new artificial intelligence model that performs on par with Claude Mythos, signaling a major shift in Asian AI capabilities.

items.title: Claude Fable 5 Expected to Launch This Week Amid Rising Enterprise Adoption
items.summary: Claude Fable 5 is anticipated to release this week, as businesses increasingly choose Claude over ChatGPT, with enterprise adoption driving measurable outcomes over mere announcements.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: GTA 6 Pre-Orders Hit 39 Million in First Day, Generating $3 Billion
items.summary: GTA 6 pre-orders launched on June 25, 2026, with reports indicating over 39 million orders and $3 billion in revenue within 24 hours, though Take-Two has not officially confirmed the figures. The game is set to release on November 19, 2026, with pre-order players able to load it starting November 12.

items.title: IO Interactive's 007: First Light Sells 1.5 Million Copies in First 24 Hours
items.summary: IO Interactive's highly anticipated James Bond action-adventure game, 007: First Light, sold 1.5 million copies within its first 24 hours of release, marking a major success for the franchise.

items.title: Xbox Challenges Claims of PlayStation Dominating GTA 6 Pre-Orders
items.summary: Xbox has publicly disputed affiliate data suggesting PlayStation is outperforming Xbox by an 8-to-1 ratio in GTA 6 pre-orders, while French retailer Cdiscount hailed the game as the biggest cultural event of 2026.

items.title: Nintendo Switch 2 Sales Drop 87% Following Price Increases, Yet Remains Best-Selling Console in 2026
items.summary: Video Game Chronicle reports that Nintendo Switch 2 hardware sales have fallen 87% after price hikes, but the console still leads overall sales in 2026, with 007: First Light projected as the top-selling new game in May.

items.title: Sony Announces New PlayStation Controller with Unexpected Design at State of Play
items.summary: During Sony's June State of Play event, the company revealed a new PlayStation controller featuring an unexpected design, alongside announcements for Nioh 3, Ninja Gaiden, and a new Marvel fighting game.

items.title: Helldivers 2 Reveals New Force of Law Warbond for Cooperative Shooter
items.summary: Helldivers 2 surprised fans by announcing the upcoming Force of Law Warbond, a new expansion for the cooperative shooter that will be available to players soon.

items.title: Former XDefiant Producer Announces Exit from Gaming Industry After One Year
items.summary: As fans say goodbye to XDefiant after just one year, the game's executive producer announced he will be stepping away from the gaming industry permanently.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: RAM Prices Skyrocket Amid Class Action Lawsuit Against Samsung, SKH, and Micron
items.summary: A class action lawsuit alleges that Samsung, SKH Highix, and Micron are fixing prices and reducing supply of DDR3 and DDR4 RAM, causing significant price increases.

items.title: Red Magic Confirms Global Launch of Gaming Tablet 5 Pro Under New Name
items.summary: Red Magic has confirmed the global launch of its Red Magic Gaming Tablet 5 Pro, previously released in China, now under a new name.

items.title: Linux Kernel 7.2 Release Candidate 1 Drops
items.summary: Linux kernel 7.2 has entered release candidate 1, marking a major step toward its stable release, with improvements for gamers and open-source developers.

items.title: CachyOS Surges in Popularity Among Gamers
items.summary: CachyOS, a Linux distribution optimized for gaming, has rapidly gained popularity, especially among gamers seeking performance enhancements.

items.title: Ayaneo Pocket Micro 2 Announced
items.summary: Ayaneo has announced the Pocket Micro 2, a new handheld gaming device with enhanced features for portable gaming enthusiasts.

items.title: PCSX2 and ARMSX2 Receive Major Updates
items.summary: PCSX2 (PlayStation 2 emulator) and ARMSX2 (Nintendo 64 emulator) have received significant updates improving compatibility and performance.

items.title: DK64 PC Port Recompilation Announced
items.summary: A new video announces the DK64 PC port recompiled, bringing the classic Nintendo 64 game to PC with enhanced performance.

items.title: China Uses Mobile LiDAR Vans for Urban Air Quality Monitoring
items.summary: China has deployed mobile LiDAR vans capable of detecting human presence and monitoring urban air quality even in darkness, fog, or smoke.
